vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely unsupported by visual evidence. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/not visible. P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable as distinct specimens. P3 (Pothos) is present but appears to be a singular, struggling cutting or remnant.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant material visible in the yellow pot.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No identifiable mint foliage present.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Present: Pothos cutting remains, though health is critical.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No identifiable silver guest foliage present.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil/substrate. No botanical growth detected.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Black pot contains soil and debris, but no viable plant structures.
plant_audit.P3: Single stem/leaf structure visible. Shows signs of severe dehydration and lack of turgor pressure.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil appears consistently dry and compacted across all pots.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or perlite) in P3 pot. A metallic object (spoon) appears in the T-4 image, and a cylindrical object (likely a sensor or marker) is present in the yellow pot.

temporal_deltas.T-2: Significant lighting drop; visibility of plant structures obscured.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Return of lighting reveals no recovery or new growth; the P3 specimen remains in a state of stasis/decline.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of extreme neglect. P3 is the only surviving specimen, and it is failing. The lack of green pigmentation and the absence of new growth suggest a total failure of the current maintenance regime.
anomalies: Introduction of a spoon in T-4.
Introduction of a sensor/marker in the yellow pot.
Presence of white particulate matter (eggshells) in P3.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is currently non-viable for the registered species. The yellow pot (P1) is empty, and the black pots (P2/P3/P4) show no signs of the expected diversity. The Pothos (P3) is the sole occupant, exhibiting signs of severe physiological stress. The presence of household items like a spoon and unidentified markers suggests human intervention, but these have not improved the health of the specimens.
